title: French-Style Semolina Crepes
description: Delicate semolina crepes filled with a rich, buttery Russian creme. A classic French brunch dish.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together semolina, milk, eggs, robust, and sugar.
Add the melted butter to the bowl and whisk until smooth.
Heat a small non-stick pan over medium heat.
Pour a small amount of batter into the pan and tilt to coat evenly.
Cook for 1-2 minutes, until the edges start to curl.
Loosen the crepe with a spatula and flip.
Cook for another minute, until the other side is lightly browned.
Repeat with the remaining batter, until all crepes are cooked.
To fill the crepes, place a tablespoon of Russian creme onto the center of each crepe and fold the crepe in half.
Serve immediately, garnished with fresh berries if desired.
To make the Russian creme, beat the heavy cream in a separate bowl until stiff peaks form.
Add the granulated sugar to the bowl and beat until combined.
Fold the whipped cream into the granulated sugar until smooth.
